title: 20.-Contract No. 32400003 Amendment: Cooke & Associates Background Investigations

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract amendment with
Cooke & Associates, a background investigation services provider for public safety
personnel, to increase the contract amount by $280,000 to a total contract of $580,000
and extend the contract term for two additional years expiring on July 31, 2028. This
amendment will allow the Berkeley Police Department (BPD) and Berkeley Fire
Department (BFD) to continue utilizing Cooke & Associates for timely, thorough, and
high-quality background investigations of candidates, and will provide the City Manager
with the authority to spend funds as needed under the amended contract to support
ongoing public safety hiring needs.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for this contract in FY 2025 are allocated in the following budget codes:
 Berkeley Police Department special fund: 126-71-702-805-0000-000-421-612990
 Berkeley Fire Department: Measure FF 164-72-742-831-0000-000-422-612990
The amendment includes $160,000 for BPD and $120,000 for BFD.
sections.current_situation:
The ability to conduct background investigations quickly and thoroughly is critical for
filling vacancies in public safety positions. Delays in hiring lead to extended vacancies,
increased workload on current staff, and reduced service capacity. Cooke & Associates
has consistently delivered high-quality, efficient investigations that support timely hiring
decisions.
Both BPD and BFD continue to experience staffing shortages and face a competitive
hiring landscape. The existing contract with Cooke & Associates has enabled both
departments to process more applicants, particularly for urgent roles such as police

May 20, 2025
officers, dispatchers, and firefighter/paramedics. This ensures we are able to identify
and select the most qualified candidates.
The original contract with Cooke & Associates, which began on May 15, 2023, was
approved for $50,000 and set to expire on May 15, 2025. In August 2023, the contract
was amended to increase the not-to-exceed amount to $300,000 and extend the term
through July 31, 2026. As of today, a total of $261,865.11 has been spent by BPD and
BFD under this contract.
While acknowledging that there is a hiring freeze in place, there will continue to be
needs for police and fire hiring, and positions will be assessed by public safety and city
manager staff for case specific decisions. Due to the success of the partnership and
ongoing hiring needs, BPD is requesting authority to spend up to an additional $160,000
and BFD is requesting an additional $120,000 to continue utilizing Cooke & Associates’
services.
sections.background:
Cooke & Associates has been a trusted vendor for the City of Berkeley, providing
detailed, timely, and POST-compliant background investigations. They have
demonstrated strong communication, flexibility, and responsiveness to department
needs, especially during high-volume recruitment periods.
Their services have helped both BPD and BFD avoid delays in academy enrollments
and onboarding processes. The proposed amendment will ensure continued support at
a time when public safety vacancy rates for the Berkeley Police Department are at
17.7% for sworn staff. For the Berkeley Fire Department, vacancies for firefighters are
at approximately 12%.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
No identifiable environmental effects or opportunities are associated with this report’s
subject.
sections.rationale:
Cooke & Associates has a proven track record with the City of Berkeley. Their
investigative work supports effective public safety hiring by ensuring thorough vetting of
candidates. The additional funding will support ongoing hiring efforts and help the City
meet current and future staffing demands.
sections.alternatives:
Relying solely on internal staff for all background investigations would extend hiring
timelines and reduce capacity during peak hiring periods. Additionally, current staffing
levels would require much of this internal work to be done on an overtime basis, making
outsourcing this work the more fiscally responsible approach.

sections.attachments:
1. Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,### N.S.
CONTRACT AMENDMENT: COOKE & ASSOCIATES FOR BACKGROUND
INVESTIGATION SERVICES FOR PUBLIC SAFETY PERSONNEL
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley continues to face critical public safety staffing needs;
and
WHEREAS, timely and comprehensive background investigations are essential to hiring
qualified personnel in law enforcement and fire services; and
WHEREAS, Cooke & Associates has provided the City of Berkeley with professional
and reliable background investigation services that meet POST standards; and
WHEREAS, the City’s existing contract with Cooke & Associates needs to be amended
to provide additional funding to support continued services for the Berkeley Police
Department and Berkeley Fire Department; and
WHEREAS, funds for this contract amendment have been allocated in the following
budget codes:
 Berkeley Police Department special fund: 126-71-702-805-0000-000-421-612990
 Berkeley Fire Department: Measure FF 164-72-742-831-0000-000-422-612990
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is hereby authorized to execute an amendment to the existing contract
with Cooke & Associates for background investigation services, increasing the contract
by $280,000, for a total not-to-exceed amount of $580,000 and extend the contract term
for two additional years to July 31, 2028.
